nodes.context: Japan's largest yakuza organization, involved in various criminal activities.
Affiliated yakuza family to the Yamaguchi-gumi.
Kobe-based faction that broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.

Faction that broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
New group formed by breakaway factions from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
Breakaway group formed in 1984 after a leadership dispute.

Family led by Shinobu Tsukasa before becoming kumichō.
Family led by Kiyoshi Takayama, wakagashira under Tsukasa.

edges.evidence_quote: Tetsuya Shiroo, a senior ranking member of the Suishin-kai (an affiliated yakuza family to the Yamaguchi-gumi)
edges.context: Suishin-kai is an affiliated family of Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Yamaken-gumi broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Takumi-gumi broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Kyoyu-kai broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: the Yamaguchi-gumi informed the Hyogo Prefectural Police of its intention to end its conflict with organizations such as the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Yamaguchi-gumi was in conflict with Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi after the split.

edges.evidence_quote: Hiroshi Yamamoto broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi with many of its powerful members to form the Ichiwa-kai. A bitter rivalry existed between the two groups, which led to an all-out war (the Yama-Ichi War)
edges.context: Ichiwa-kai broke away and fought a war with Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Masahisa Takenaka (kumichō of the Takenaka-gumi) to be the fourth kumichō of Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Takenaka-gumi is a subsidiary family within Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Hiroshi Yamamoto (kumichō of the Yamahiro-gumi), broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Yamahiro-gumi was a faction that broke away to form Ichiwa-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: acting-kumichō Kazuo Nakanishi (kumichō of the Nakanishi-gumi)
edges.context: Nakanishi-gumi is a subsidiary family within Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: wakagashira-hosa Shinobu Tsukasa (then kumichō of the Hirota-gumi) was chosen as new wakagashira
edges.context: Hirota-gumi is a subsidiary family within Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Kiyoshi Takayama, kumichō of the Kodo-kai, was elected as wakagashira
edges.context: Kodo-kai is a subsidiary family within Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: They absorbed the Tokyo-based gang Kokusui-kai
edges.context: Yamaguchi-gumi absorbed Kokusui-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Inagawa-kai, Soai-kai, Matsuba-kai, Kyōsei-kai, Aizukotetsu-kai, Toa-kai, Fukuhaku-kai, Gōda-ikka, Shinwa-kai
edges.context: Inagawa-kai is an ally of Yamaguchi-gumi.
